resources.string: This Android build is being ported for full feature parity with the live iOS and web versions of Whack A Braille. The app will stay TalkBack-first and support external keyboards, Perkins input, TalkBack braille entry, and external braille displays.
Starter navigation has been removed. The next steps are porting the Kotlin game core, Android speech and audio services, and the accessible gameplay screens.

Standard keyboard means an external hardware keyboard. Perkins mode maps S, D, F, J, K, and L to dots 3, 2, 1, 4, 5, and 6. Braille text entry will support TalkBack Braille Keyboard, and braille display mode will support translated input, 8-dot braille, and HID-style interaction when available.
